Step 5 — Create the first table + RLS policies (Supabase SQL editor)
Use Clerk’s proven pattern: `user_id` defaults to `auth.jwt()->>'sub'` and policies compare to that subject claim. Clerk+1
create table if not exists public.tasks (
user_id text not null default auth.jwt()->>'sub'
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
alter table public.tasks enable row level security;
drop policy if exists "User can view their own tasks" on public.tasks;
create policy "User can view their own tasks"
drop policy if exists "Users must insert their own tasks" on public.tasks;
create policy "Users must insert their own tasks"
Callout: Supabase positions RLS as “defense in depth” and recommends using it to protect data even when accessed via external tooling. Supabase
